primaryQuestion: Will future Ingredient Identity changes require recipe architecture rewrites?
answer: NO — not if recipes bind to Ingredient Concept (Option A binding). Adapter refactor in costing resolver only.

recommendations.separation: Recipes = culinary ontology; Purchasing = commercial contracts + price_history

1_startPrepSubRecipesNow.question: Can Marginly safely start Prep/Sub-Recipes now?
1_startPrepSubRecipesNow.answer: YES — with costing caveats for multi-format concepts
conditions: Bind recipe lines to ingredient_id (concept) only
Use resolveRecipeLineOperationalCost / recipe-merge — never raw current_price in UI
Accept costing drift on multi-format concepts until P1 default_pack_variant

2_identityBlockerForRecipeArchitecture.question: Is Ingredient Identity a blocker for Recipe Architecture?
2_identityBlockerForRecipeArchitecture.answer: NO for prep/sub-recipe structure; PARTIAL for production-grade costing accuracy
2_identityBlockerForRecipeArchitecture.detail: sub_recipe_id XOR graph, output_quantity, prep propagation — ready now. Trustworthy €/line on multi-supplier catalogs needs P0+P1.

3_majorRewriteProbability.question: Probability recipe work today requires major rewrites after Pack Variants?
3_majorRewriteProbability.answer: 12% major rewrite if concept binding + resolver pattern followed; 45% if pack_variant required on every line; 85% if supplier_product binding

4_nextDevSession.question: Next dev session: Ingredient Identity OR Prep/Sub-Recipes?
4_nextDevSession.answer: P0 Ingredient Identity guard FIRST (1–2 days), then Prep/Sub-Recipes in parallel with P1
rationale: Aligns with ingredient-identity-future-design: P0 stops OI false signals
P0 is small and does not block prep structural work
Prep graph can proceed while P1 schema is built
Do not enable recipe cost alerts tied to price_history until P0+P1
sequencing: After P1: wire recipe resolver to default_pack_variant

vlValidation.mozzarella: Purchasing/history only — no recipe schema change
vlValidation.pepino: Purchasing/matching only — recipe uses conserva concept
vlValidation.gingerBeer: Purchasing/normalization only — recipe uses beverage concept

headline: Recipe architecture is compatible with Option E. Start prep/sub-recipes now on concept binding; ship P0 identity guard first; expect resolver adapter not rewrite when pack variants land.
